Hanshin Pocha | Korean comfort food
top of page
NEVER BEEN
CLOSED
SINCE 1998
WHAT IS POCHA?
Representative of Korean indoor snack stalls.
From the 1980s to the late 1990s, street food wagons gained popularity as an option
to serve food on a budget to people who are hungry after a long day of work.
The outdoor food wagons evolved into semi-outdoor stalls which we know as 'Pocha' today.
To the people of this era, Hanshin Pocha was not only a place to satisfy their hunger,
but also a
place to find companies and entertainment.
LOCATIONS
LA - CA
Rowland Heights - CA
Oakland - CA
Mesa - AZ
Irvine - CA
Austin - TX
Duluth - GA
San Diego - CA
